text.title: Multi-Tenancy Patterns — Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the multi-tenancy ADR shape + isolation-guarantee contract. Architect or supporting agent producing the decision must satisfy these contracts.

forbidden.pattern: isolation_guarantees.guaranteed_no field empty (every model has trade-offs; if "none not guaranteed", you're lying) (r2)
blast_radius cells with vague values ("minimal", "low") instead of tenants_affected count + time values (r3)
migration_path field empty or "will figure out if needed" (r4)
verification_test absent (the guarantee is unverifiable, so untrustworthy) (r2)
operational_burden missing (the choice's true cost is unmeasured)

allowed-transformations.transformation: Choose "hybrid" (e.g., shared-everything for free tier, DB-per-tenant for enterprise) — document the boundary criterion and the operational implications of both.
Reference a parent architecture document for any guarantee that applies across multiple ADRs (e.g., "key custody per pro/infra/devops-engineer/key-management-foundations").
Defer specific implementation details (RLS rules, schema naming) to sub-ADRs IF the parent decision is final and the sub-decisions are well-scoped.

list.item: Decision derived from requirements_scored, not preference (r1)
Blast radius has numeric tenant counts + RTO + RPO per failure class (r3)
Migration path documented with explicit trigger + phases (r4)
Data residency explicit per default + per-tenant override capability (r5)
Operational burden estimated (DBA hours, monitoring, backups)
